description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and tool definitions — enables elementStore to be used as a Claude Code plugin. The mcp:server agent auto-discovers classes and generates MCP tools.

classes.name: MCP Server
classes.description: An MCP server instance that exposes elementStore as tools to AI assistants. Tracks connection config and discovery state.

props.label: ElementStore URL
props.description: Base URL of the elementStore instance this server connects to
Whether to expose system classes (@class, @prop, etc.) as tools
Regex or glob pattern to filter which classes get tools (empty = all)
Class IDs discovered on last connection

classes.name: MCP Tool
classes.description: A single MCP tool generated from an elementStore class or action. Tracks the mapping between ES operations and MCP tool names.

props.description: The MCP server this tool belongs to
The elementStore class this tool was generated from (null for generic tools)
The CRUD operation this tool performs
JSON Schema for the tool input parameters

classes.name: MCP Agent
classes.description: An AI agent specialized for MCP server management — discovers classes, generates tools, manages server lifecycle.

props.description: The MCP server this agent manages
Whether to auto-discover classes on startup

seed.name: elementStore Default
seed.description: Default MCP server for the local elementStore instance

seed.name: MCP Bridge
seed.title: MCP Server Agent
seed.description: Manages the MCP server lifecycle — discovers elementStore classes, generates MCP tool definitions, and keeps the tool registry in sync with the store schema.
seed.prompt:
You are the MCP Bridge agent. Your job is to keep the MCP server's tool definitions in sync with the elementStore schema.

## Your Domain
- Discover all elementStore classes and their properties
- Generate MCP tool definitions for each class (list, get, create, update, delete)
- Register generic tools (query, find, health, classes, class_props, action)
- Track which classes have been mapped and their tool status

## On Each Run
1. Query all classes from elementStore: GET /class
2. For each class, get props: GET /class/{id}/props
3. Generate tool definitions using the tool-generator
4. Update mcp:tool objects to reflect current state
5. Update mcp:server with discovery timestamp and counts
